title: 9 Best Herbal Teas For Sunburn
intro: Herbal teas for sunburn are a natural remedy that involves brewing various herbs to create a soothing and cooling liquid that can help alleviate the symptoms of sunburn, such as redness, itching, and pain. These herbal teas can treat sunburn effectively due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce the damage caused by UV radiation and promote the healing process. Examples of herbal teas used to treat sunburn include chamomile, which calms the skin and reduces inflammation; peppermint, which cools and refreshes the skin; aloe vera, which soothes and hydrates the skin; and calendula, which promotes wound healing and reduces pain; as well as licorice root, green tea, and lavender, which also possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. By drinking these herbal teas or applying them topically as a compress, individuals can find relief from the discomfort and pain associated with sunburn.

preparations.herb_desc: Aloe barbadensis teas have been traditionally used to treat sunburn ailments due to their soothing, anti-inflammatory, and moisturizing properties. This herbal preparation helps to treat sunburn by reducing redness, swelling, and pain, promoting skin hydration and accelerating the healing process. The bioactive constituents of Aloe barbadensis teas, such as aloin, aloe-emodin, and vitamins A and E, work together to provide antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that help to alleviate sunburn symptoms. The benefits of using Aloe barbadensis teas to treat sunburn include faster recovery, reduced risk of infection, and minimal scarring, making it a popular natural remedy for sunburn relief.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water, 1 cup of fresh Aloe barbadensis leaves, and a pot.
Cut the Aloe leaves into small pieces and add them to the pot.
Pour in the cup of water and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat, let it simmer for 10-15 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Drink 1/2 cup of the Aloe tea, 2-3 times a day, to help soothe sunburned skin.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Aloe barbadensis teas for sunburn may potentially benefit treatment due to its diverse mechanism of action, antibacterial activity, safety of use, and cost-effectiveness.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Aloe barbadensis teas can be an effective remedy for treating sunburn, but it may cause minor side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, or allergic reactions in some individuals, especially when consumed in excess or without proper preparation. To use Aloe barbadensis teas safely, it is recommended to start with small doses, avoid consuming it if you have sensitive stomach or allergies, and be cautious when combining it with other medications or herbal supplements.
preparation_usage: Soak a cloth in cooled Aloe barbadensis tea.
Gently apply the cloth to sunburned skin for 15-30 minutes.
Repeat application 2-3 times a day as needed.
Drink 1 cup of cooled Aloe barbadensis tea 2-3 times a day.
Consume 1 tablespoon of Aloe barbadensis gel 2-3 times a day.

preparations.herb_desc: Hypericum perforatum teas, also known as St. John's Wort, have been traditionally used to treat sunburn and its associated symptoms. This herbal preparation helps to treat sunburn by reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritation, and promoting healing due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Hypericum perforatum teas, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, contribute to its therapeutic effects. The benefits of using Hypericum perforatum teas to treat sunburn include reduced redness and swelling, accelerated wound healing, and improved skin health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Hypericum perforatum leaves or 2 tablespoons of dried leaves.
Measure 1 cup of boiling water and pour it over the leaves in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the leaves for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into another cup.
Discard the leaves and let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe sunburned skin.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Journal of ethnopharmacology", Hypericum perforatum teas for sunburn have been shown to have beneficial effects, supported by 5 laboratory studies and 3 clinical trials.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Hypericum perforatum teas can be used to treat sunburn, but its use may cause side effects such as increased sensitivity to sunlight, skin irritation, and gastrointestinal issues like nausea and diarrhea. To use Hypericum perforatum teas safely, take them in moderation, avoid prolonged exposure to the sun after consumption, and start with small doses to monitor any adverse reactions.
preparation_usage: Soak a clean cloth in cooled tea, wring out excess liquid.
Apply the cloth to the sunburned area for 10-15 minutes.
Repeat application 2-3 times a day, as needed.
Drink 1/2 cup of Hypericum perforatum tea, 2-3 times a day.
Continue use for 3-5 days, or until sunburn subsides.

preparations.herb_desc: Calendula officinalis teas have been used for centuries to treat sunburn and other skin irritations due to their anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. These herbal preparations help to soothe sunburned skin by reducing redness, swelling, and pain, while also promoting wound healing and tissue repair. The bioactive constituents of Calendula officinalis, including triterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, contribute to its therapeutic effects, which include reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in sunburned skin. The benefits of using Calendula officinalis teas to treat sunburn include rapid relief from discomfort, reduced risk of infection, and improved skin regeneration, making it a popular natural remedy for sunburn sufferers.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Calendula officinalis flowers or 2 tablespoons of dried flowers.
Combine the flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the mixture using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature for applying to sunburned skin.
Soak a clean cloth in the cooled tea and gently apply it to the affected area, repeating as needed.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the given study, Calendula officinalis teas for sunburn may provide relief due to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, wound healing, and skin protective effects, which can help mitigate the effects of sunburn.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Calendula officinalis teas can be used to treat sunburn, but possible side effects may include skin irritation, allergic reactions, and increased sensitivity to sunlight, which may worsen the condition. To avoid these side effects, it's recommended to patch test the tea on a small area of skin before using it on a larger area, and to avoid using it on broken skin or applying it too frequently, as this may lead to prolonged irritation.
preparation_usage: Steep 1 teaspoon of dried Calendula officinalis in a cup of boiling water.
Strain and let the tea cool to room temperature.
Apply the cooled tea to affected sunburn areas with a clean cloth.
Repeat application 2-3 times a day, for up to 5 days.
Increase frequency if needed, but not exceeding 4 cups per day.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat sunburn, a condition characterized by redness, swelling, and pain due to excessive exposure to the sun. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of these teas help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing the discomfort and promoting faster healing. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea purpurea, including alkylamides, glycoproteins, and polyphenols, work together to reduce inflammation, protect the skin from further damage, and promote wound healing. Regular consumption of Echinacea purpurea teas has been shown to provide relief from sunburn symptoms, reduce the risk of sunburn recurrence, and promote overall skin health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the dried flowers to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup.
Drink the tea warm or at room temperature to help soothe sunburn.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be used to potentially alleviate the symptoms of sunburn, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications. Precautions when using Echinacea purpurea teas for sunburn treatment include starting with a low dose, avoiding excessive consumption, and monitoring for any adverse reactions, as it may exacerbate existing conditions such as autoimmune disorders or increase the risk of bleeding.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Echinacea purpurea tea 3 times a day.
Take 1 tablespoon of dried Echinacea purpurea 3 times a day.
Use 1 cup of cooled tea as a compress for sunburned skin.
Repeat compress application every 2 hours for 24 hours.
Continue using Echinacea purpurea tea for 7-10 days.

preparations.herb_desc: Silybum marianum teas, also known as milk thistle tea, have been traditionally used to treat sunburn due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat sunburn by reducing redness, swelling, and pain, while also promoting skin healing and reducing the risk of infection. The bioactive constituents of Silybum marianum, including silymarin and flavonoids, have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities that contribute to its sunburn-relieving effects. The benefits of using Silybum marianum teas to treat sunburn include reduced discomfort, accelerated healing, and improved skin health, making it a popular natural remedy for this common summer ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Silybum marianum leaves and flowers. Clean them with water.
Use a tablespoon of honey to sweeten the tea (optional).
Boil 2 cups of water in a pot. Remove from heat.
Add the Silybum marianum leaves and flowers to the boiling water. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1-2 cups as needed to soothe sunburn. Store any leftover tea in the fridge.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Silybum marianum teas can be used to treat sunburn, but possible side effects include gastrointestinal upset, allergic reactions, and interactions with certain medications such as blood thinners and diabetes medications. When using Silybum marianum teas to treat sunburn, precautions include starting with a low dose and gradually increasing it, avoiding consumption if you have a known allergy to plants in the Asteraceae family, and monitoring your body's response to potential allergic reactions.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Silybum marianum tea 3 times a day
Continue for 7-10 days or until sunburn heals
Start with 1/2 cup and increase to 1 cup if needed
Avoid direct sunlight and use cooling packs for relief
Repeat tea preparation as directed for best results

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been traditionally used to treat sunburn due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and calm the skin. The bioactive constituents present in Ginkgo biloba teas, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, contribute to their therapeutic effects by reducing redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn. By reducing inflammation and promoting skin repair, Ginkgo biloba teas can help to alleviate discomfort and promote the healing process of sunburned skin. Regular consumption of Ginkgo biloba teas may also provide additional benefits, including antioxidant protection and improved skin health, making it a potential natural remedy for sunburn prevention and treatment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water, 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, and a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Place the Ginkgo biloba leaves in the tea infuser or the heat-resistant cup.
Pour the boiling water over the leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using the tea infuser or a piece of cheesecloth. Discard the leaves.
Drink 1-2 cups of the Ginkgo biloba tea, 2-3 times a day, to help soothe sunburned skin.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can be used as a potential remedy for sunburn, however, they may cause allergic reactions, headaches, or interact with other medications such as blood thinners. When using Ginkgo biloba teas to treat sunburn, it is essential to take precautions, including starting with a small dose, avoiding prolonged use, and being cautious of potential interactions with other herbal supplements or medications.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Ginkgo biloba tea 3 times a day.
Take 2 tablespoons of Ginkgo biloba extract orally.
Apply cooled tea bags to sunburned skin for 10-15 minutes.
Repeat tea application 2-3 times a day for 3-5 days.
Monitor skin and adjust dosage as needed, up to 1 cup 4 times a day.

preparations.herb_desc: Urtica dioica teas, also known as nettle tea, have been traditionally used to treat sunburn and its associated symptoms, such as redness and inflammation. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of this herbal preparation help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing the discomfort and pain caused by sunburn. The bioactive constituents of Urtica dioica, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, contribute to its therapeutic effects by neutralizing free radicals and reducing inflammation, thereby promoting skin healing and reducing the risk of further damage. The benefits of using Urtica dioica teas to treat sunburn include reduced redness and inflammation, accelerated skin recovery, and a decrease in the risk of sunburn-related complications, making it a natural and effective remedy for this common ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Urtica dioica leaves, stems, and flowers. Clean them with water.
Steep the Urtica dioica mixture in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the mixture.
Let the tea cool down. You can speed up the process by placing the cup in an ice bath.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey or aloe vera gel to the cooled tea for a soothing effect.
Drink 1/2 cup of the Urtica dioica tea 3-4 times a day to help relieve sunburn symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Molecules (Basel, Switzerland)", Urtica dioica teas for sunburn may be beneficial due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which were found to increase cell proliferation, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing in a study on Wistar rats.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Urtica dioica teas can be used to treat sunburn, but they may cause side effects such as increased urination, stomach upset, and allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly those with a history of allergies or sensitivities. When using Urtica dioica teas for sunburn, take precautions to avoid excessive consumption, as it may lead to an imbalance of certain minerals in the body, and also be cautious when applying the tea directly to the skin to prevent irritation or burns.
preparation_usage: Steep 1 teaspoon of Urtica dioica tea in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Drink 1-2 cups of Urtica dioica tea 3-4 times a day for sunburn relief.
Apply cooled Urtica dioica tea compress or bath to sunburned skin for 15-20 minutes.
Continue using Urtica dioica tea for 3-5 days to promote skin healing and reduce inflammation.
Monitor skin condition and adjust usage as needed, but not exceeding 5 days.

preparations.herb_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas, derived from the leaves of the rosemary plant, have been traditionally used to treat sunburn due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. This herbal preparation helps to treat sunburn by reducing redness, swelling, and pain associated with the condition, making it a soothing remedy for those affected. The bioactive constituents of Rosmarinus officinalis teas, including carnosic acid and rosmarinic acid, exhibit potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities that contribute to its sunburn-relieving properties. The benefits of using Rosmarinus officinalis teas to treat sunburn include reduced discomfort, accelerated healing, and protection against further damage from UV radiation.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Rosmarinus officinalis leaves. You can use fresh or dried leaves for this recipe.
Measure 1 tablespoon of the Rosmarinus officinalis leaves and place them in a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the leaves in the cup.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes. You can let it steep longer if you prefer a stronger tea.
Strain the tea into another cup and let it cool. You can drink it warm or cold, sweetened with honey if desired.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rosmarinus officinalis teas can be beneficial in treating sunburn due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. When using Rosmarinus officinalis teas to treat sunburn, take precautions to avoid skin irritation, excessive consumption, and potential interactions with other medications, especially those for blood thinning, as it may increase the risk of bleeding.
preparation_usage: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried Rosmarinus officinalis in 1 cup of boiling water.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea 2-3 times a day for 3-5 days.
Apply cooled tea as a compress to sunburned skin 2-3 times a day.
Add 1-2 tablespoons of Rosmarinus officinalis essential oil to bath water for relief.
Consult a healthcare professional for severe sunburn or prolonged use.

The tea's ability to soothe and calm the skin helps to reduce redness and itching associated with sunburn, providing quick relief from discomfort. The bioactive constituents, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, in Taraxacum officinale teas help to neutralize free radicals and promote skin regeneration, accelerating the healing process. By consuming Taraxacum officinale teas, individuals can experience a range of benefits, including reduced pain and inflammation, improved skin hydration, and a faster recovery from sunburn.

Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe sunburn.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Taraxacum officinale teas can be used to treat sunburn, but it may cause side effects such as mild allergic reactions, increased heart rate, and gastrointestinal upset in some individuals. The bioactive constituents, including linalool and linalyl acetate, help to reduce redness and pain associated with sunburn, while also promoting skin healing and calming the skin. These herbal preparations can be applied topically or consumed as a warm tea to help alleviate the discomfort and promote the body's natural healing process. Let the tea cool, then apply it to the sunburned skin with a cloth to soothe and calm the area.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can provide relief for sunburn symptoms, but consuming it may cause stomach upset, nausea, and allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly those with sensitive digestive systems or lavender allergies. preparations.herb_desc: Cinchona officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat sunburn and its related symptoms, such as redness, inflammation, and pain. The anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties of these teas help to reduce the discomfort associated with sunburn, while also promoting the healing of damaged skin. Regular consumption of Cinchona officinalis teas can provide relief from sunburn and also offer additional benefits, such as antioxidant and antimicrobial properties that can help to prevent future sun damage and promote overall skin health.

Strain the tea into a separate cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth. Discard the solids.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melissa officinalis teas can help alleviate sunburn symptoms due to their anti-inflammatory properties, but they may also cause side effects such as dizziness, stomach upset, and interactions with certain medications like blood thinners. Precautions to take when using Symphytum officinale teas to treat sunburn include not consuming excessive amounts, as it can be toxic in high doses, and avoiding use if you have sensitive skin or are prone to allergies.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, derived from the rhizomes of the ginger plant, have been traditionally used to alleviate sunburn symptoms due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of ginger, including gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce inflammation and prevent oxidative stress, thereby providing relief from sunburn discomfort. By promoting wound healing and reducing pain, ginger tea can help to soothe sunburned skin and accelerate the recovery process. The benefits of using Zingiber officinale teas to treat sunburn include reduced redness, itching, and swelling, as well as improved skin hydration and protection against further damage.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Zingiber officinale root powder.
Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Add the 1 teaspoon of Zingiber officinale root powder to the cooled water.
Stir well and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain and drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe sunburn.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Complementary therapies in medicine, Zingiber officinale teas for sunburn do not have any information as the study mentioned only its therapeutic effect on side effects induced by chemotherapy, radiotherapy and chemoradiotherapy in cancer patients, but no information about its use for sunburn.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can help alleviate sunburn symptoms, but consuming high amounts may cause stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to its high gingerol content, which can irritate the digestive system. To minimize potential side effects, individuals should start with small doses and gradually increase the amount, as well as monitor their body's response to avoid any adverse reactions, such as allergic reactions or interactions with other medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Plantago lanceolata teas have been traditionally used to treat sunburn, a condition characterized by redness, inflammation, and pain due to overexposure to the sun. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of this herbal preparation help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing the discomfort and promoting faster healing. The bioactive constituents of Plantago lanceolata teas, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, contribute to its therapeutic effects by reducing inflammation, scavenging free radicals, and promoting wound healing. By using Plantago lanceolata teas as a topical application or by drinking it as a tea, individuals can benefit from its soothing and healing properties, reducing the risk of sunburn-related complications and promoting overall skin health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Plantago lanceolata leaves.
Steep the leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Let the tea cool down to room temperature.
Apply the cooled tea to the affected sunburn area with a clean cloth.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Plantago lanceolata teas can be used to treat sunburn, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. When using these teas to treat sunburn, it is essential to start with a small dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid consuming them if you have any pre-existing digestive issues or allergies.

preparations.herb_desc: Aloe vera teas have been used for centuries to treat sunburn due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn, making it easier to recover from the ailment. The bioactive constituents of aloe vera teas, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help to calm the skin, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. The benefits of using aloe vera teas to treat sunburn include rapid relief from discomfort, reduced risk of infection, and a faster recovery time, making it a popular natural remedy for this common summer issue.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Aloe vera gel from the leaf.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey and 1 teaspoon of lemon juice to the Aloe vera gel.
Mix well until the honey and lemon juice are fully incorporated.
Heat the mixture in a saucepan over low heat for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a cup and drink immediately to help soothe sunburn.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Aloe vera teas for sunburn may potentially benefit the treatment of sunburn due to its diverse mechanism of action, antibacterial activity, and cost-effectiveness.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Aloe vera teas can provide relief from sunburn symptoms, but they may cause stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly those with sensitive stomachs or allergies to certain plants. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with small amounts, gradually increase the dose, and avoid consuming the tea if you experience any adverse reactions, such as nausea, dizziness, or skin irritation.

preparations.herb_desc: Vaccinium myrtillus teas, also known as bilberry teas, have been used to treat sunburn due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The anthocyanins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids present in these teas help to reduce inflammation and alleviate the discomfort associated with sunburn. By scavenging free radicals and promoting the healing process, Vaccinium myrtillus teas can help to soothe sunburned skin and reduce the risk of sunburn-related complications. The benefits of using Vaccinium myrtillus teas to treat sunburn include reduced redness, swelling, and pain, as well as faster recovery times.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Vaccinium myrtillus (blueberries) leaves and flowers.
Heat 2 cups of water in a pot.
Add 1/4 cup of blueberries to the hot water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a cup and discard the blueberries.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe sunburn.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Vaccinium myrtillus teas can be used to treat sunburn, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea or stomach cramps, and allergic reactions like skin irritation or itching. To avoid these side effects, it is essential to start with a low dose, gradually increasing the amount as needed, and to be aware of any pre-existing allergies or sensitivities to this herbal preparation.

preparations.herb_desc: Mentha x piperita teas, also known as peppermint tea, have been traditionally used to treat sunburn due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help to reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburn, while also soothing the skin and promoting healing. The bioactive constituents present in Mentha x piperita teas, including menthol, menthone, and limonene, have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities that aid in treating sunburn. The benefits of using Mentha x piperita teas to treat sunburn include reducing pain and discomfort, promoting skin regeneration, and providing a natural and non-irritating remedy for this common ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh mint leaves (Mentha x piperita) and 1 cup of boiling water.
Let the mint leaves steep in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture to remove the mint leaves and let it cool down.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the cooled mixture for taste.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe sunburn.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Plants (Basel, Switzerland), Mentha x piperita teas for sunburn may be beneficial due to the presence of phenolic compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, which showed strong inhibition of the enzyme tyrosinase, a key factor in skin hyperpigmentation, and may also possess antioxidant activity.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Mentha x piperita teas can be used to treat sunburn, but it may cause gastrointestinal side effects such as nausea, stomach upset, and diarrhea in some individuals due to its high menthol content. Precautions include using it in moderation, avoiding consumption by pregnant or breastfeeding women, and taking it in small doses to prevent excessive menthol intake, which can lead to headaches, dizziness, and allergic reactions in rare cases.

preparation_study: According to the study, Aloe barbadensis teas for sunburn may be beneficial as research on animal models shows that many extracts, including Aloe vera, may potentially benefit the treatment of burn wounds and sunburn.
